name: [TEMPLATE] DT Ingest — Open-Meteo weather + air quality

meta.templateNote: Template/spec only — import into n8n, attach your own local Postgres credential, then activate. Contains no credentials or secrets. Open-Meteo requires no API key.

parameters.content:
TEMPLATE — Open-Meteo ingestion

1. Runs hourly.
2. Opens an ingestion_runs row (status=running) keyed by the n8n execution id, so retries are idempotent.
3. Fetches hourly weather + air quality for each station in 'Define stations'.
4. Upserts geo_entities and observations via the natural-key UNIQUE constraints (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING / DO UPDATE).
5. Closes the run as success, or as error via the error branch — failed runs are recorded, never silently dropped.

Before activating: create a Postgres credential in your n8n instance and select it on every Postgres node. Do NOT commit credentials to this repository.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO digital_twin.ingestion_runs (source_id, run_key, status)
VALUES ('open-meteo', $1, 'running')
ON CONFLICT (source_id, run_key) DO UPDATE SET status = 'running', error_message = NULL
RETURNING id;

postgres.name: REPLACE WITH YOUR LOCAL POSTGRES CREDENTIAL

parameters.query:
WITH ent AS (
  INSERT INTO digital_twin.geo_entities (source_id, external_id, kind, name, lon, lat)
  VALUES ($1, $2, 'station', $3, $4, $5)
  ON CONFLICT (source_id, external_id) DO UPDATE SET updated_at = now()
  RETURNING id
)
INSERT INTO digital_twin.observations (source_id, entity_id, layer, external_ref, observed_at, lon, lat, values)
SELECT $1, ent.id, $6, $2, $7::timestamptz, $4, $5, $8::jsonb FROM ent
ON CONFLICT (source_id, layer, external_ref, observed_at) DO NOTHING;
